observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their
respective property records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE.
Should reconciliation of the inventory report and accounting records appeared to be
improbable, consider availing the one-time cleansing of PPE in accordance
with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and
b. Municipal General Services Officer to use and properly accomplish the prescribed
format of RPCPPE in the conduct and reporting of the annual physical count of
PPE provided in Section 57 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services
Officer and Municipal Accountant to propose a plan to locate the source documents
and old records of PPE items amounting to P4,294,010.35 to facilitate the preparation
of correct subsidiary records and if all efforts proved futile, to study and consider
availing of the provisions of the one-time cleansing of the PPE balances under COA
Circular 2020-006 so that proper and necessary reconciliation, disposition,
derecognition and adjustments in the PPE accounts may be made accordingly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make
the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to fairly present the balances of
the affected accounts in the SFPo and henceforth, set-up RPT and SET Receivable
accounts at the beginning of the year based on the certified list of taxpayers and the
amount due and collectible for the year established from the Real Property Tax
Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. submit to the Audit Team all necessary documents such as Billing Statements,
Report of Accomplishments and other pertinent documents to prove the validity
of the recorded Accounts Payable, otherwise make the necessary adjustments in
the books of accounts to fairly present the balances of the affected accounts in
the financial statements; and
b. refrain from recording Accounts Payable merely on the basis of obligations in
Obligation Requests, and instead, ensure that all relevant supporting documents
are attached to the claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Health Officer to submit promptly all necessary documents to the Municipal
Accounting Office to facilitate the timely recording of the receipts and utilization of
donations in kind; and
b. Municipal Accounting Office to record all receipts of donations in kind and its
subsequent utilization in the books of accounts pursuant to pertinent provisions of
the MNGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Engineer to submit to the Audit Team a written
explanation/justification, supported with all pertinent documents, as to the non-
completion of the Construction of Diversion Road at Market to Bucal, Tanza,
Cavite, project monitoring reports from the assigned Project Engineers, if there is
any;
b. Municipal Engineer to formulate and implement an action plan to fast-track the
completion of the project costing P19,742,528.78 and thereafter, ensure strict
compliance with the guidelines on the issuance of suspension order provided
under the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 to avoid unnecessary delay in the
implementation of infrastructure projects, otherwise liquidated damages shall be
imposed against the contractor;
c. Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to
ensure that necessary detailed engineering activities are conducted prior to
procurement of infrastructure projects; and
d. BAC to require the submission of certification from the MEO that detailed
engineering surveys and designs have been conducted by the latter.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineering Office to
conduct preliminary inspection and submit punch-lists to contractors on
infrastructure projects when they reach an accomplishment of ninety-five percent
(95%) of the total contract amount to determine defects and thus, make necessary
corrections, before proper turnover and acceptance of the projects to the
Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator, Municipal Engineer, BAC Chairman and other concerned
officials to thoroughly plan the start and target completion dates and timetable of the
procurement of all development projects and ensure that all issues that may affect
the implementation of a project are discussed and resolved prior to the projects’
inclusion in the APP and AIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to submit to the Office of the Auditor
the copy of monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th
day after the end of each month, as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002
dated September 12, 2012, to facilitate the timely evaluation of the implementation of
disaster risk management activities and early communication to Management of the
noted deficiencies, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make
the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to fairly present the balances of
the affected accounts in the SFPo and immediately return the funds to the Bureau of
Treasury.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct responsible officials to
explain to him in writing, copy furnish the Audit Team for validation, on why no action
was taken by them to ensure that the funds were utilized as intended.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure
that payments are made directly to payee/supplier through the issuance of checks,
except those expressly allowed to be paid through cash advances under the
aforementioned Circular, to safeguard funds from loss or misuse and ensure that
appropriate taxes are withheld by the Municipality as withholding agent of the
government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the disposal team to facilitate the
immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties, including the determination of
the appropriate mode of disposal and appraisal of the same to prevent further
deterioration and reduction of the properties’ economic value.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and other
concerned officials to prepare and submit the necessary requirements to enable the
Municipality to include in its automated payroll system the payment of the wages of
its Job Order workers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to intensify the review and reconciliation of their respective property records through in-depth verification and analysis of PPE accounts and identify the cause/s of the noted deficiencies and necessary adjustments in their respective records.

observations.managementAction: Necessary journal entries were made by the Municipal Accountant under JEV No. 100-2022-12-16027 dated December 31, 2022 copy furnished the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a Schedule of Unreleased Checks at the end of the year and submit it to the Municipal Accountant who, on the basis thereof, shall draw the JV for the restoration of cash equivalent of the unreleased checks and recognition of the appropriate payable/liability accounts and in addition, reverse the entries made in the ensuing year, in compliance with Section 79 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer already complied with the requirements in the restoration of cash equivalent for the unreleased checks and recognition of the appropriate payable/liability accounts prescribed under Section 79 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume 1

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to collect additional business taxes of P539,370.93 plus penalties and surcharges, if provided under the Municipality’s Revised Revenue Code, otherwise corresponding Notice of Charge will be issued by the COA Audit Team.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer had collected additional payment of business taxes including penalties totaling P540,518.47 under OR No. 6375433 dated September 2, 2022; OR No. 6572978 dated December 29, 2022; OR No. 6161431 dated June 15, 2022; OR No. 6550106 dated October 5, 2022; and OR No. 6372841 dated August 19, 2022.
from the concerned five suppliers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to verify the discrepancy in the balances of Due from and Due to LGUs of the Municipality with that of its reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province and make the necessary adjustments. We further recommended the Municipal Accountant to request the Provincial Accountant to furnish her with copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers showing the amount collected by the Province in favor of the Municipality to serve as basis in recording the Due from LGUs in the books of the Municipality as provided for under Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant was able to confirm from the Provincial Accountant that the balances of Due From and Due to LGUs of the Municipality are equal with the reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province. These were supported by Subsidiary Ledger from the Province.
JEV No. 100-2022-12-16021 and JEV No. 200-2022-12-0036, both dated December 29, 2022, were prepared by Municipal Accountant showing the amount collected by the Province in favor of the Municipality. These were supported by Subsidiary Ledger from the Province.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Health Officer and General Services Officer in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to submit to the COA Audit Team all the Acceptance and Inspection Reports pertinent to the subject transactions, otherwise the payments will be suspended or disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Acceptance and Inspection Reports pertinent already submitted to the COA Audit Team by the Municipal Accountant on June 13, 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Health Officer to submit immediately to the Audit Team the aforementioned documents to establish the payment of AHDP and SRA for the covering period September 15, 2020 to December 19, 2020 with total amount of P313,539.26. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments for AHDP and SRA are supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted to the COA Audit Team the duly approved payroll signed by the authorized recipients, approved DTR and certification of actual number of days reported for work, signed by the HR and head of health facility on June 13, 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials to ensure that the information in the project signboards and/or public notices displayed at the project location such as the project duration, start date of construction, target date of completion, cost incurred, percentage of completion as of given date are complete and up-to-date.
observations.managementAction: submitted the photos of the on-going and completed projects showing the signboard displayed at the job site with complete information.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the GFPS Focal Person, and the Technical Working Group (TWG) to submit a catch-up plan on GAD which shall include: a. assessment of the training needs of all officials and employees primarily the involved on GAD and needs of all officials and employees primarily involved on GAD. b. effective scheduling and monitoring of the implementation of the GAD PPAs in the GPB to ensure that the gender issues identified therein are correspondingly addressed; and c. enhancement of the Municipality’s database that could also serve as GAD database by including/gathering sex-disaggregated data and GAD related information.
observations.managementAction:
Management has started assessing the training needs of all officials and employees primarily the involved on GAD and needs of all officials and employees primarily involved on GAD.
Effective scheduling and monitoring of the implementation of the GAD PPAs in the GPB to ensure that the gender issues identified therein are correspondingly addressed.
Database stationed at the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) was updated and used already for gathering sex disaggregated data in the preparation of GPB CYs 2023 and 2024.
observations.reason: Management is still assessing the training needs and updating the database for full compliance.

non-existing/missing PPE items for the one-time cleansing of PPE account

dated January 31, 2020.

restoration of cash equivalent of the unreleased checks and recognition of the

the GPB to ensure that the gender issues identified therein are correspondingly

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: GSO and Committee to conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of all property and equipment of the Municipality at least once a year and prepare and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Audit Team before January 31 of the immediate succeeding year.
observations.managementAction: Inventory submitted RPCPPE.
observations.reason: Municipality was not conducted by the Inventory Committee specifically Land Improvements, Infrastructure Assets and Buildings and Other Structures under the Special Education Fund. Reiterated in Observation No. 1, Page No. 36, Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accounting Office with the duly certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the current year and thereafter to enable the Municipal Accountant to correctly set up the RPT and SET Receivables for the current and succeeding years; b. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of the RPT and SET Receivables and their reciprocal accounts as of December 31, 2020 with the total delinquency tax report submitted by the Municipal Treasurer and make the necessary adjustments in their records.
observations.managementAction: Summary of Certified List of Delinquent Taxpayers with total amount due was submitted by the Municipal Treasurer on June 11, 2021. The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer reconciled the balances of the RPT and SET Receivables and their reciprocal accounts as of December 31, 2020 with the total delinquency tax report submitted by the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. MSWDO to submit to the Audit Team an explanation on why two beneficiaries were given more than P5,000.00 or justification on why Case Study Reports were not prepared in those cases, otherwise the amounts will be suspended in audit; b. MSWDO and other concerned Municipal officials to submit the Case Study; c. MSWDO to prepare specific guidelines, for review, approval and indorsement of the Municipal Mayor to the Sangguniang Bayan, providing the eligibility requirements and uniform financial assistance to be given for each type of medical case, in conformity with the DSWD MC No. 11 series of 2019 as amended by DSWD MC No. 24 series of 2020; d. Assigned social worker and the MSWDO to ensure the completeness and correctness of the documentary requirements submitted by the applicant/beneficiary before the release of assistance; and e. Municipal Accountant to see to it that all the necessary supporting documents are complete and properly accomplished before processing claims for the payment.
observations.managementAction: 24 copies of SACs and Certifications were submitted. A Case Study Summary of beneficiaries was submitted by MSWDO. MSWDO prepared and submitted a copy of Case Study. The Office of the Municipal Accountant verified that all the supporting documents were submitted before the processing of claims for payment.
observations.reason: 264 more SACs and 294 documents and 60 Certifications were not submitted. Most of the beneficiaries submitted supporting documents with missing information. Some beneficiaries could not be validated due to unreliable or inactive contact numbers; some returned to their provinces.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the copies of the Minutes of Meetings and other related documents of the pre-procurement and pre-bid conference, Minutes of Pre-bid Conference dated November 26, 2019, Minutes of Pre-bid Conference dated December 11, 2019 and Notice of Bidding Results to non-winning bidder, and a statement that all the procurement undertaken by the Municipality are properly documented, duly signed and notarized; b. Municipal Accountant and BAC Secretariat to submit copies of the eligibility requirements of the PhilGEPS Registration, BIR Tax Certificate, BIR Certificate of Registration, Certificate of Environmental Compliance (ECC), Mayor’s permit, and updated audited Financial Statements and Annual Income Tax Return were submitted; c. MENRO to institute controls to ensure the veracity of the declared dates the disposal and hauling services were rendered such as requiring the assigned personnel to include in the Record of Attendance/Service the Time IN and Time OUT, Truck Plate Number and signature of the driver during hauling and collection of waste of the Municipality; and d. MENRO to require the contractor to prepare the Statement of Account for each month billings signed by the company President or his duly authorized representative.
observations.managementAction: Copy of Minutes of Pre-Procurement Conference dated November 26, 2019, Minutes of Pre-bid Conference dated December 11, 2019 and Notice of Bidding Results to non-winning bidder were submitted by the Municipal Accountant and BAC Secretariat. Copy of SEC Registration, BIR Certificate, BIR Tax Certificate, Mayor’s permit, and Omnibus Sworn Statement of the suppliers were submitted. Record of Time IN and OUT and Transfer Station Acknowledgment Receipts were maintained by the MENRO. A Monthly Statement of Account prepared by the contractor was submitted.
observations.reason: Verified that all supporting documents and requirements were submitted. Evidence that some Minutes were not submitted earlier but later provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. The Municipal Budget Officer to provide written explanation and/or justification on why the subject expenditures amounting to P387,880.00 were charged against the LDRRMF despite being not related to disaster risk management related programs and activities to prevent suspension or/and disallowance of the transactions in audit; b. Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that only expenditures directly related to disaster risk management are charged against the LDRRMF; and c. The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to identify the projects and incorporate, under a separate caption, in the LDRRMFIP the projects and activities to be charged out of the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer provided explanation that the amount was charged to Transportation Equipment of MOOE instead of LDRRMF. The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (MDRRMO) submitted a copy of LDRRMFIP for the previous years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to explain to the Audit Team, for further evaluation, why the various procurements were made in favor of Tanza Employees MPC, and whether the procurements should be suspended or disallowed in audit; and that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned municipal officials ensure that all supporting documents and required information of the procurements are complete to facilitate the auditorial review whether procurement in favor of Tanza Employees MPC comply with procurement rules and to prevent suspension of payments in audit.
observations.managementAction: As justified by the BAC, the supplier to whom the procurement was made in favor of Tanza Employees MPC, the BAC decided not to participate in the procurement request of the Municipality. The Office of the Municipal Accountant verified that all the supporting documents werecomplete before the processing of payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee to explain/justify the discrepancy between the price of the rice commodity procured by the Municipality in response to the COVID-19 and the suggested retail price issued by the authorized government agency during the price freeze observation or request reconsideration on the audit observation.
observations.managementAction: BAC explained that the procurement was made under Emergency of Heal as One Act and prices were justified by the municipality due to availability and urgency. Proof that NOAs and POs were posted in GPPB Online Portal was later submitted.
observations.reason: As justified by the municipal officials, the procurement was due to emergency needs and supplier availability. Request for reconsideration on the audit observation was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. MSWDO to submit to the Audit Team the social amelioration cards, photocopies of identification cards with specimen signatures and relevant certificates required under Item VII of DSWD MC No. 2020-04 and valid documents required to support the payments of beneficiaries, otherwise the transactions will be suspended in audit; b. MSWDO to coordinate with Barangay officials to facilitate the submission to the Audit Team of the lacking documents and updated contact numbers of beneficiaries for further validation; and c. Investigate further and provide justification for the occurrence of the social amelioration cards with no SAC Numbers (bar code) and case name that did not tie up with the names in the Master List provided by the MSWDO.
observations.managementAction: Contact numbers of 27 beneficiaries were provided. Most of the required documents and bar codes were provided by the MSWDO. The MSWDO submitted the SAC forms and explained some beneficiaries returned funds due to subsidies they received from other government agencies.
observations.reason: Of sample beneficiaries, some could not be validated due to unreliable or inactive contact numbers or had returned to their provinces. Some supporting documents were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. BAC Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team justifications on its inability to comply with the posting requirement under GPPB 01-2020; b. BAC and the Mayor to ensure that Mayor’s permit, Income/Business Tax Return and Omnibus Sworn Statement of the suppliers pertaining to COVID-19 expenditures are submitted to avoid issuance of audit suspension; and c. Municipal Accountant to facilitate the immediate submission to the Audit Team of the lacking documents and/or correction of deficiencies noted in audit to prevent suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: BAC Secretariat submitted justifications that some procurements were undertaken under Heal as One Act and explanation on non-posting to GPPB Online Portal; NOAs and POs were later posted. Municipal Accountant facilitated submission of Mayor’s permit, Income/Business Tax Return and Omnibus Sworn Statement for suppliers. Lacking supporting documents were later submitted.
observations.reason: Some NOAs and POs were subsequently posted; some supplier documents were only later provided; initially some documents were deficient which caused partial implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Limit the grant of cash advances to Municipal officials and employees only to circumstances allowed under COA Circular No. 97-002 or when payment thru checks will not be practicable to ensure that government funds are safeguarded from risk of loss or misappropriation and applicable taxes due from government money payments to suppliers/creditors are withheld pursuant to BIR RMC No. 23-2014; b. The Municipal Accountant to facilitate the submission to the Audit Team of the list of recipients of relief goods with signature, photos and other documenting documents and ensure correction of deficiencies; and c. The concerned municipal officials to justify instances where Sales Invoice/Official Receipt were dated prior to the grant of cash advances to avoid issuance of suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: No cash advance was granted for COVID-19 expenditures in CY 2021. List of recipients of relief goods with documentation were submitted. Municipal Health Office justified instances where Sales Invoice/Official Receipt dated prior to the cash advance due to urgent delivery.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to expedite the establishment and maintenance of a comprehensive GAD Database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and b. GFPS Focal Person and GFPS TWG to ensure that only programs, projects and activities that address gender issues and concerns are included in the GPB and that the requirements for GPB preparation and submission are complied with.
observations.managementAction: GFPS reasoned that sex disaggregated data was based on the database of the concerned office who submitted gender issues and concern. DILG scheduled GAD trainings which will be attended by the GFPS and GFPS Technical Working Group (TWG).
observations.reason: Sex disaggregated data was based on databases of concerned offices and CBMS data needed update. Trainings were scheduled but GAD analysis using HGDG tool was not yet conducted; GPB still lacked some required elements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE of the Municipality to include those which are located outside the premises of the Municipal Hall and submit the RPCPPE to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year; and
b) Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to continue the reconciliation of their respective records to establish the correctness and reliability of the balances of the PPE accounts reflected in the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee was not able to complete the physical count of all PPE owned by the Municipality, hence no RPCPPE was submitted to the Audit Team for the period ending December 31, 2020.
observations.reason: Due To frequent lockdowns and other measures implemented by the Municipality the Inventory Committee was not able to complete the physical count. No RPCPPE was implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to
conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of all property and equipment of the
Municipality at least once a year and prepare and submit the corresponding
RPCPPE to the Audit Team on or before January 31 of the immediate succeeding
year;

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accounting Office with the duly
certified list showing the names of all taxpayers and the amount due and
collectible for the year 2021 and every year thereafter to enable the Municipal
Accountant to correctly set up the RPT and SET Receivables for the current
and succeeding years; and
b. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of the RPT and SET
Receivables and their reciprocal accounts as of December 31, 2020 with the
total delinquency tax reported by the Municipal Treasurer and make the
necessary adjustments in their records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Welfare and Social Development Officer (MWSDO) to submit to the
Audit Team an explanation on why two beneficiaries were declared eligible for
assistance as they were COVID-19 positive and were granted P10,000.00 each
even though official test results showed otherwise, to prevent suspension or
disallowance of the amount in audit;
b. MSWDO and other concerned Municipal officials to submit the Case Study
Reports or Case Summary of beneficiaries who were given more than P5,000.00
or justification on why Case Study Reports were not prepared in those cases,
otherwise the amounts will be suspended in audit;
c. MSWDO to prepare specific guidelines, for review, approval and indorsement
of the Municipal Mayor to the Sangguniang Bayan, providing the eligibility
requirements and uniform financial assistance to be given for each type of
medical case, in conformity with the DSWD MC No. 11 series of 2019 as
amended by DSWD MC No 24, series of 2020,
d. Assigned social worker and the MSWDO to ensure the completeness and
correctness of the documentary requirements submitted by the applicant/
beneficiary before the release of assistance; and
e. Municipal Accountant to see to it that all the necessary supporting documents
are complete and properly accomplished before processing claims for
payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to revisit the contract for
amendments or preparation of a supplemental contract, whichever is
applicable and permissible, to include the vital information enumerated in COA
Circular No. 2012-001;
b) Municipal Accountant and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to
submit to the Audit Team the copies of the Minutes of Meetings and other
related bidding conferences conducted during the procurement process such
as pre-procurement and pre-bid conference, Notice of Bidding Results to non-
winning bidder, and a statement that all the procurement undertaken by the
Municipality are properly documented, duly signed and notarized, to prevent the
suspension of payments in audit;
c) Municipal Accountant and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to
submit to the Audit Team copies of the eligibility requirements of the
contractor/service provider to prevent suspension of the payments in audit;
d) Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) to institute
controls to ensure the veracity of the declared dates the disposal and hauling
services were rendered such as requiring the assigned personnel to include in
the Record of Attendance/Service of SB HAIN ENTERPRISES & GENERAL
SERVICES INC., the Time In, Time Out, and Signature of Driver prepared by the
Municipality’s representative and Certified Correct by the MENRO and ensuring
that the driver had also signed the Acknowledgement Receipt; and
e) MENRO to require the contractor, to prepare the Statement of Account for each
month billings signed by the company President or his duly authorized
representative.

Henceforth, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant and other
concerned officials ensure that all supporting documents and required
information are complete to facilitate the auditorial review of contract, technical
evaluation of garbage collection and disposal services and post-audit of
disbursements, otherwise the transactions will be suspended in audit.

on why the subject expenditures amounting to P387,880.00 were charged
against the LDRRMF despite being not related to disaster risk reduction and
management activities to prevent suspension or/and disallowance of the
transactions in audit;
b. The Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that only expenditures directly related
to disaster risk management are charged against the LDRRMF; and
c. The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to identify and
incorporate, under a separate caption, in the LDRRMFIP the projects and
activities to be charged out of the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards
Committee to explain to the Audit Team, for further evaluation, why various
procurement of the Municipality made in favor of Tanza Employees MPC should
not be suspended or disallowed in audit due to violation of the conflict of interest
provisions of GPPB Circular No. 08-2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee
to explain/justify the material discrepancy between the price of the rice
commodity procured by the Municipality in response to the COVID-19 and the
suggested retail price issued by the authorized government agency during the
price freeze implementation, to prevent suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

Team the social amelioration cards, photocopies of identification cards with
specimen signatures and relevant certifications required under Item VII of
DSWD MC No. 2020-04 or valid justifications, for evaluation of the Audit Team,
on why the documents could not be produced, otherwise the transactions will
be suspended in audit;
b. MSWDO to coordinate with concerned Barangay officials to facilitate the
submission to the Audit Team of the updated contact numbers of
beneficiaries, for its further or expanded confirmation; and
c. Investigate further and provide justification for the occurrence of social
amelioration cards with no SAC Numbers (bar code) and case of name that did
not tie up with the names in the Master List provided by the MSWDO.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

evaluation;
b) BAC and the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the Mayor’s

c) The Municipal Accountant to facilitate the immediate submission to the Audit
Team the lacking documents and/or correction of deficiencies noted in audit to
prevent suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Limit the grant of cash advances to Municipal officials and employees only to
circumstances allowed under COA Circular No. 97-002 or when payment thru
checks will not be practicable to ensure that government funds are safeguarded
from risk of loss or misappropriation and applicable taxes due from government
money payments to suppliers/creditors are withheld pursuant to BIR RMC
No. 23-2014;
b. The Municipal Accountant to facilitate the immediate submission to the Audit
Team the lacking documents and/or correction of deficiencies noted in audit to
prevent suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions; and
c. The concerned municipal officials to justify the instances were the Sales
Invoice/Official Receipts were dated prior to the grant of cash advances to
avoid issuance of suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to expedite the

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all inventory items and prepare and submit the corresponding Inventory Report of the Physical Count of Inventories to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Disposal Committee to spearhead the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with the policies and procedures provided in the Manual on Supply and Property Management in the Local Governments prescribed under COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
observations.managementAction: The Disposal Committee facilitated the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties.

The Municipality in cooperation with the forty one (41) Punong Barangays conducted a meeting with the Municipal Mayor to discuss the campaign programs of the Municipality. It was reiterated in the meeting the campaign programs. In addition, tarpaulins were posted in conspicuous places in different barangays for public awareness.

The Office of the Municipal Engineer implemented the last phase of construction of the Sanitary Landfill project the implementation phase which is the electrification which will be completed on February 21, 2021. Also, the Municipality is awaiting for the approval of Sanitary Landfill’s electrical application with Meralco.

Management is considering the replacement of the shredder machine.

MENRO monitored the waste diversion through submission of ECA Quarterly Report and Barangay MRS Report of all barangays.

The Municipality programmed and implemented the establishment of six (6) MRF in different barangay. Moreover, the strict implementation of the “No Segregation, No Collection” policy had implemented.
observations.reason: Due to the pandemic, the Office of the Municipal Engineer experienced delay in the implementation. Also, the Municipality is awaiting for the approval of Sanitary Landfill’s electrical application with Meralco. Management is considering the replacement of the machine. The lack of space in most barangays prevented the establishment of MRF.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the: 
a) OIC, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to provide definite timetables or schedules of CY 2020 implementation of all the projects and activities in the LDRRMFIP for proper monitoring and evaluation of its implementation;

c) ensure that all the projects, programs and activities to be included in the LDRRMFIP are in accordance with RA 10121 and NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-002 dated March 25, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMFIP for CY 2020 provided definite timetables for the implementation of programs, projects and activities including project locations. Supplemental LDRRMIP was adopted to re-program the funds to new projects, programs and activities that will response to Public Health Emergency of COVID-19.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the: 
a) Officer-in-Charge (OIC), Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to ensure that all information that will facilitate the timely and effective implementation of projects such as the location of the projects and specific schedules of their implementation are incorporated in the 20 per cent Development Fund Annual Investment Plan; and 
b) Municipal Development Council and OIC-MPDC to review the unimplemented projects covered by the continuing appropriation to determine whether they are still feasible and if not, re-programmed in accordance with existing budgeting and accounting rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: A proposal related to Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system was presented by the Landbank officials during its meeting with the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer. The proposal is still under review and negotiation.
observations.reason: Implementation on the use of ATM payroll system was delayed due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) require the Bids and Awards Committee to: a) post in the PhilGEPS website, website of the Municipality, and at the conspicuous place reserved in the premises of the Municipality for a period of calendar days the RFQs for goods/supplies costing more than P50,000.00 whenever Small Value Procurement is resorted to by the Municipality; b) require the suppliers/contractors to submit all documentary requirements for the alternative mode of procurement and indicate in the RFQ in what stage of the procurement process these requirements must be submitted, that is on the filling-out of the offer/s, evaluation offer/s, before issuance of Notice of Award or prior to payment; and c) ensure that the RFQs contain all the necessary information such as the complete specifications of the items, ABC for the items, the exact addresses of the suppliers, and the name and signature of the authorized official or the employee requesting the quotations.
observations.managementAction: The BAC posted in three conspicuous places the RFQs for procurement of goods/supplies. However, most of the procurements in CY 2020 were done through negotiation to meet the immediate needs during the pandemic; hence the documentary requirement for the posting of notices of awards, RFQs, etc. were overlooked.
observations.reason: Most of the procurements in CY 2020 were done thru negotiation to meet the immediate needs during the pandemic, hence the documentary requirement for the posting of notices of awards, RFQs, etc. were overlooked.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineering Office, the BAC Secretariat and the Office of the Municipal General Services to furnish the Audit Team with copies of the approved contracts, purchase orders and all the documents forming part thereof within five days from their execution as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to facilitate prompt review and evaluation of the contracts and early communication to Management of the deficiencies, if any.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Engineer, the BAC Secretariat and the Office of the Municipal General Services furnished the Audit Team the copies of approved contracts, purchase orders and all the required documents within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General Services Office to ensure that the serial number to be assigned to the PO is unique to each PO and does not include a letter-suffix to avoid the impression that the Municipality is deviating from the normal sequence of procurement activities, specifically that POs are prepared, processed and approved only after deliveries, acceptance and/or inspection of the items had already been made.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal General Services ensured that the serial number assigned to the PO is unique to each PO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services Officer to:
a. have all service vehicles owned by the Municipality be marked with “For Official Use Only” which should be written the name of the Municipal Government of Tanza to properly identify the vehicles as government property; and
b. secure government plates for the 31 government motor vehicles.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the General Services ensured that the service vehicles of the Municipality were marked with “For Official Use Only”. The Office of the General Services received additional five (5) government plates from the Land Transportation Office.
observations.reason: The remaining government plates were still pending for release by the Land Transportation Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Sangguniang Bayan speed up the formulation of the guidelines and the terms and conditions of lease and operation of the Tanza Business Center for it to be fully operational and generate income for the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to consider the audit recommendation in their planning activities
observations.reason: The guidelines covering the operation of Tanza Business Center and the terms and conditions of lease as well as the nature of businesses it will cater to were not formulated as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Municipal Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) to ensure that the daily attendance of all employees, including JOs, be captured using the biometric machine and that the computer-generated DTRs based on the captured biometric data be used as basis for payroll preparation; and
b) All JOs, except those assigned in the field, be required to use the same biometric machine or bundy clock to establish a more reliable record of the number of days that they rendered work as the basis for the computation of their wages.
observations.managementAction:
Testing conducted on the use of the biometric machines revealed that the machines were not capable of handling huge number of employees, hence the Municipality is looking for possible system that can accommodate the needs of the Municipality in terms of number of employees and the physical layout of the whole Municipal Complex.
The Rural Health Unit and the Market Office will be using the old system in order to utilize the earlier procured biometric machines.
observations.reason: The plan to acquire a new system of recording attendance will be considered and anticipated funding requirement caused delay; the new system was not yet procured as of CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned Municipal officials require the surveyor to expedite the approval of the survey plan within the time granted. Otherwise, return the unexpended balance to the HUDCC for the amount to be available for other priority programs of the said source agency.
observations.managementAction: The Management is considering the return of the unexpended balance to the HUDCC, due to the failure of the surveyor to complete the survey plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to capacitate the personnel involved in the GAD Program, foremost of them are the GFPS-TWG, by sending them to attend relevant trainings and workshops and thereafter, direct the immediate setting up of a GAD Database that will contain statistics and sex disaggregated data systematically gathered and regularly updated to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation and subjected to gender analysis, in compliance with Section 4.B, Item 1 of JMC No. 2013-01 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that they will comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Completion of the CBMS (intended source of GAD Database) was not yet finished; the CBMS process of development was suspended in April 2018 due to Barangay Election and updating is currently on-going.

observations.managementAction:

Physical inventory of PPE located outside the Municipal Hall premises were not carried out.
Difficulty in retrieval of records from Calendar Year 2015 and earlier.

Charge (OIC), Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to:

projects and activities in the LDRRMFIP for proper guidance and monitoring
of their implementation; and
b) Review the programs of the unexpended balances and determine whether
these are still feasible, otherwise, re-program the funds allotted for such
projects by submitting a revised plan upon the recommendation of the Local
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council and approval by the
Sangguniang Bayan and the Municipal Mayor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

Sangguniang Bayan approving the release and use of P4,354,763.95 to
purchase various relief goods for distribution chargeable against the 30 per
cent QRF of the Municipality. Henceforth, adherence to paragraph 6.6.3 of the
NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 is enjoined to ensure proper use of
public funds;
b) MDRRMO and Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) to
provide to the Audit Team with the proofs of receipts of the welfare goods
indicating therein the actual number of items received per
beneficiaries/recipients to attest to the veracity of the fund utilization and avoid
the issuance of Notice of Suspension. Henceforth, all assistance extended or

that the resources of the government are managed and utilized in accordance
with the established rules and regulations; and

the utilization of the LDRRMF prescribed under COA Circular Nos. 2014-002 and

the formulation of the guidelines covering the management and operation of the
Tanza Business Center for it to be fully operational and generate income for the

strictly implement the no segregation/no collection policy and comply with all

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor procure additional biometric
machines that could accommodate all employees of the Municipality. Thereafter,

basis for their preparation of payrolls. We further recommended that all JOs,
except those assigned in the field, be required to use the same machine or bundy
clock to establish a more reliable record of the number of days that they rendered
work as the basis for the computation of their wages.

require the surveyor to expedite the approval of the survey plan within the time

Give priority on the implementation of the construction, repair and maintenance of school buildings and facilities for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries.
observations.reason: For CY 2016, expenses amounting to P63,700.00 not related to the intended purpose of SEF was still paid by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the created GFPS to set up and maintain GAD Database to serve as basis for a gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.reason: The gathering of data to organize into GAD database was not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Municipal officials to fully comply with the basic requirements of the RA 9003 or the Solid Waste Management Act of 2000. The local Sangguniang Bayan should enact ordinances that will increase the awareness and cooperation of the constituents on proper solid waste management such as rules on the mandatory waste segregation at source.
observations.reason: The Municipality still unable to fully comply with the basic requirements of RA No. 9003 and still obtained very low compliance level.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
Accountant and the General  Services  Officer  to  prioritize  the  complete
reconciliation of their PPE records to ascertain the reliability,  existence
and correctness of the  PPE  accounts.   Moreover,  require  the  Accounting
Office and the GSO to maintain a complete and updated PPE Ledger  Cards  and
Property Cards, respectively, to reconcile the balances of the accounts,  in
compliance with Section 375(d) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the   Municipal
Treasurer to prepare and submit a certified list of names of  the  taxpayers
with the amount of taxes due  and  collectible  based  on  the  RPT  Account
Register/Taxpayer’s Index Cards,  which  will  be  the  basis  of  the  City
Accountant in setting-up the receivables at the beginning of  the  year,  to
establish with certainty the reported RPT and SET Receivables  as  of  year-
end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Municipal
officials to  facilitate  the  installation  of  electricity  in  the  newly
constructed Tanza Business Center so that its stalls will be leased out  and
revenues will be immediately generated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal DRRMO and
the other concerned implementing offices  to  fully  utilize  the  fund  and
implement all budgeted projects, programs  and  activities.   The  Municipal
DRRMO further be required to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report  on
the Sources and  Utilization  of  the  Funds,  certified  by  the  Municipal
Accountant, for proper and timely evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Municipal
officials to fully comply with the basic requirements of the RA 9003 or  the
Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.   The  Sannguniang  Bayan  should  enact
ordinances  that  will  increase  the  awareness  and  cooperation  of   the
constituents  on  proper  solid  waste  management  such  as  rules  on  the
mandatory waste segregation at source.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the Municipal
Mayor instruct the created GFPS to set  up  and  maintain  GAD  Database  to
serve as basis for a  gender-responsive  planning,  programming  and  policy
formulation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and  the  Local  School  Board
ensure that only projects and programs enumerated in Section 272 of RA  7160
and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No.  01-B  dated  June  25,  2001  are
charged against the  SEF.   Give  priority  on  the  implementation  of  the
construction, repair and maintenance of school buildings and facilities  for
the benefit of the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and  all  concerned  Municipal
Officials to submit  to  the  Sangguniang  Bayan  all  disbursement  charged
against Other Developments Fund supported  with  specific  programs/projects
for approval. Henceforth, incorporate in  the  Annual  Budget  the  specific
programs/projects to be charged against the Fund in accordance with  Article
410 of the IRR of RA 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and  the  concerned  Municipal
officials to require the surveyor to expedite the  approval  of  the  survey
plan  as  the  fund  has  already  been  idle  for  more  than  five  years.
Otherwise, return the unutilized amount to  the  grantor  to  program  other
priority development programs of the national government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned  Municipal
officials submit written justification why the said  expenditures  need  not
be  disallowed  in  audit,  for  further  evaluation  of  the  Audit   Team.
Henceforth, strictly adhere with the provisions of DILG Memorandum  Circular
No. 2011-59.  Stop undertaking similar activities in places outside  of  the
official stations or territorial  jurisdictions  funded  out  of  government
funds since there is available venue in the Municipal  Hall  Building.   The
Local Chief Executive should likewise take full cognizance of the  principal
responsibility and accountability relative to  the  prudent  use  of  scarce
financial resources.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop granting assistance to 38
Punong Barangays for payment for the cost of  hired  motor  vehicles  during
their monthly meetings in the Municipal Hall.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the   concerned
beneficiaries/recipients  to  submit  acknowledgement   receipts,   or   its
equivalent, on the mentioned donated items  to  confirm  actual  acceptance.
Moreover,  require  to  submit  the  lacking  letter  request  and  deed  of
donation.   Likewise,   the   Municipal   Mayor   should    stop    granting
donation/assistance for private purpose in accordance   Section 4(2)  of  PD
No. 1445.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  OIC-Municipal
Engineer to submit the basis for the computation of  ABC  and  justification
for non compliance with the aforementioned BIR regulation  for  our  further
evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC  Secretary  and
the General Services Officer to  submit  to  COA  copies  of  contracts  and
Purchase  Orders  together  with  their  supporting  documents  within  five
working days from execution/issuance, respectively, to  facilitate  their
immediate review and evaluation  in  accordance  with  the  above  mentioned
regulations.

We recommended  that  the  General  Services  Office  responsible  for
accepting deliveries notify COA of  the  time  and  date  of  the  scheduled
deliveries for ocular verification of the existence  and  condition  of  the
supplies, materials and equipment. In addition,  copies  of  Inspection  and
Acceptance Reports should also be submitted within 24 hours upon  acceptance
as mandated by the regulation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Secretariat to
post all Notices of Award in the PhilGEPS within the prescribed  date/period
together with the Notices to Proceed and  the  approved  contracts  for  the
procurement of goods, services and  infrastructure  projects  in  conformity
with Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the   Municipal
Accountant to maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund and prepare  the
financial reports with  the  necessary  supporting  schedules  for  economic
enterprises  and  development  projects.  Accordingly,  separate   financial
statements for the economic enterprises and  development  projects    should
be prepared at year-end to form part of the schedule  of  the  General  Fund
Trial Balance as mandated under Section 313 of RA 7160 and  Section  110  of
the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.
We recommended further that the Local School Board utilize the SEF for purposes mandated under Section 272 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The agency already returned the investment in time deposit to the current account of the SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials and employees to always see to it that the payments for supplies and materials as well as other operating expenses are made directly to the suppliers. Limit payments through reimbursement and deduct/withhold the mandatory taxes. Strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 93 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason: The Municipality was able to lessen/limited payments through reimbursement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to deduct the ten percent (10%) retention money on the total amount due the contractor from every progress billing made or 10% of the 50% of the total contract cost, in compliance with the above mentioned regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal accountant already deducted the 10% retention money from every progress payments made to the contractors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to strictly comply with the posting of public notice on all infrastructure projects to promote public awareness in the use of government funds. We further recommended that the OIC-Municipal Engineer inform the Audit Team that appropriate signboards and/or public notices were already posted at the project site and in conspicuous places within the agency premises.
observations.managementAction: Compliance on the postings of public notice were already implemented, however, some information on the signboards were not indicated.
observations.reason: The notifications to the Audit team were submitted quarterly instead of monthly.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that henceforth, the OIC-Municipal Accountant ensure that the granting of 15% advance payment to contractor are recorded using the appropriate account. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entry to correct the overstatement of Construction in Progress-Infrastructure Assets and the understatement of the Advances to Contractor accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already posted the appropriate Adjusting Journal Entry. The granting of 15% advance payment made to contractors were already recorded by the Municipal Accountant using the appropriate account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Engineer to explain his failure to monitor the completion of the project and his failure to recommend appropriate courses of action provided under Item 8.4 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. Likewise, require the contractor to pay the corresponding liquidated damages due to the unjustified delayed in the completion of the project.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Engineer submitted the letter request of the contractor for the suspension of the project including the approval of the then Municipal Mayor

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-HRM Officer to adjust the leave cards/records of the concerned personnel by exhausting first the vacation leave balances.

We recommended henceforth, that management limit the granting of regular monetization of vacation leave credits within the limits of ten (10) days to a maximum of thirty (30) days within a year, pursuant to Section 22 of CSC Memorandum Circular No. 41, s. 1998.
observations.managementAction:
The leave cards/records of the concerned employees were already adjusted.

The Municipality already limit the granting of vacation leave credits to ten days.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and concerned officials strictly comply with Revenue Regulations No. 5-2011. Compensation not among those considered as “de minimis benefits” should be subjected to income/withholding tax.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2016, all overtime payments granted to the Municipal employees were subjected to withholding tax.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant not to process claims that are not properly documented with the necessary supporting documents. Support the disbursement vouchers with documentary evidence showing that the financial and material assistance were used/utilized for public purpose such as Official Receipt from the LNB, projects/activities to be undertaken and Delivery Receipt with acknowledgment of the barangay.
observations.managementAction: The concerned Municipal officials already submitted the lacking supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Budget Officer to ensure that only expenditures/projects covered under the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 are considered in the charging against the 20% Development Fund. Moreover, strict adherence to the abovementioned guidelines on the utilization of the Development Fund should be observed. We also recommended that expenses of this nature may be charged instead against the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2016, projects covered under the priority development projects enumerated in the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01 of the DILG-DBM are considered in the charging against 20% Development Fund. Moreover, strict adherence to the abovementioned guidelines on the utilization of the Development Fund should be observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRM Officer to prepare an LDRRMFIP that contain specific and not generic projects to facilitate proper evaluation as to the utilization of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMFIP already contained specific rather than generic projects.
